purpose: Provides a testable credit cycle timing tool by linking fiscal consolidation (via Kalecki) to Minsky instability thresholds, predicting that fiscal withdrawal triggers credit cycle downshift within 4-6 quarters.
components: Kalecki profit channel (fiscal deficit → corporate profits)
Credit cycle acceleration trigger (fiscal consolidation >2pp GDP)
current_assessment: The most falsifiable prediction across all agents: fiscal consolidation >2pp GDP should trigger credit cycle downshift within 4-6 quarters. Currently untriggered as fiscal remains expansionary. The maturity wall concentrates refinancing risk in the same window as potential fiscal consolidation events.
limitations: The 4-6 quarter lag is a rough estimate, not precisely calibrated
Fiscal trajectory timing is inherently unpredictable (political)
Private credit opacity may delay or obscure the credit cycle signal
The framework assumes the credit cycle is the binding constraint, which may not hold if fiscal remains dominant
